steps.title: Find the y-intercept
steps.content:
The y-intercept occurs where $x = 0$. Substitute into the equation:
$$y = 600(2)^{2(0)} = 600(2)^0 = 600 \cdot 1 = 600$$
So the y-intercept is the point $(0, 600)$, meaning 600 subscribers when $x = 0$.

steps.title: Interpret the starting time
steps.content: The problem states the equation models subscribers starting from the end of March 1998. This means $x = 0$ corresponds to the end of March 1998. At that time, there were 600 subscribers. B is correct.
